notes: Apple is perennial; stages are calendar-based, not days-after-sowing. The start_das/end_das fields encode day-of-perennial-cycle counted from March 1 = day 1 through end of February = day 365, so the existing stage_definition schema (continuity + non-overlap on integer day bounds) applies unchanged. Date ranges per stage are sourced from 'Master data Apple Insect pest.xlsx' (Crop Age column), which is internally consistent across all 10 pests x 4 stages (40 rows). The Dec-Feb window is uncovered by the source sheet and is named 'dormancy' (standard horticultural term for the leafless, no-active-growth period when pruning, dormant sprays, and chilling-hour accumulation occur).

stages.description: Bud break and shoot extension. New leaves expand and the canopy rebuilds after dormancy. High nitrogen demand begins.
Bloom and pollination. Pink bud through petal fall. Critical period for pollinator activity and frost risk.
Fruit set, cell division, and fruit expansion. Highest water and nutrient demand of the season; primary pest pressure window.
Fruit maturation, colour development, and harvest. Cultivar-specific pick windows fall inside this range.

Leafless dormancy. No active growth. Window for pruning, dormant-season sprays, and chilling-hour accumulation. Pest sheet does not cover this period; advisory engines should return dormancy-appropriate guidance (pruning, dormant spray) rather than active pest/nutrition recommendations.
